payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Intent:
Decide via ADR whether Temporal, Restate, or DBOS should replace parts of the durable job stack, with only a disabled report-only Temporal submit spike.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: PRD is well-formed with explicit public boundaries, allowed/forbidden outcomes, and Out-of-Scope anti-goals
All four promises (P1 four-option ADR, P2 disabled-by-default, P3 exactly-once submit+reattach, P4 replace-vs-stay) verified against current source
Report-only intent enforced: default engine hand_rolled, spike RuntimeError-guarded, no temporalio import, default_runtime_changed=False

criteria: P1 ADR scores all four options incl keep-hand-rolled: PASS (0004:43-116)
P2 config defaults hand_rolled + spike disabled + no temporalio import: PASS (config.py:149, spike:78, grep empty)
P3 spike id=idempotency_key/USE_EXISTING + real Layer-0 comparison non-vacuous: PASS (spike:85-86, state.py:829)
P4 ADR names replace + stay boundaries preserving gates/ledger: PASS (0004:139-166)
Artifact sha256 integrity: NOT VERIFIED (shasum approval denied -> self_reported)
Test execution: NOT RUN (test_status unknown -> self_reported per policy)
evidence: accept: prd_review gate should advance — PRD well-formed (clear promises, public boundaries, allowed/forbidden outcomes, Out-of-Scope anti-goals) and every promise maps to a real matching source boundary satisfying the report-only intent
confidence.rationale: All four PRD promises verified against on-disk source with matching boundaries and a non-vacuous comparison test referencing a real State method with exact signature; report-only enforced and no Temporal SDK import. Confidence held below 0.95 because shasum artifact-integrity (approval-denied) and pytest are un-run, both self_reported per packet policy.

payload.content: prd_review ACCEPT. All four PRD promise contracts map to real, matching source boundaries for the report-only durable-execution-engine ADR slice. P1: ADR scores 4 options (keep/Temporal/Restate/DBOS) on 6 criteria (0004:43-116). P2: config defaults engine=hand_rolled/temporal_spike_enabled=False (config.py:149), spike RuntimeError guard (:78), no temporalio import in repo. P3: spike id=idempotency_key + USE_EXISTING (:85-86), dup returns same handle (test:74), comparison test exercises real State.reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job (state.py:829, signature matches). P4: ADR names replace (:139-152) and stay-hand-rolled (:154-166) boundaries preserving gates/reviewer/artifacts/ledger.

assumptions_to_verify: The 4 spike tests pass when executed (asserted GREEN but un-run here)
On-disk planning artifacts match the packet sha256 values (self_reported)
tests/test_durable_execution_engine_adr.py asserts ADR section presence as implied by P1/P4
contradictions_checked: PRD P2 forbids importing a Temporal SDK -> grep for temporalio returned no files: consistent
P3 forbids new workflow id on retry -> FakeTemporalClient setdefault keeps same id, created=False on retry, spike marks reattached: consistent
P3 requires real SQLite Layer-0 path -> test calls State.reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job whose signature at state.py:829 matches the call exactly: consistent
Intent 'only a disabled report-only spike' -> config default hand_rolled + RuntimeError guard + default_runtime_changed=False: consistent
missing_evidence: shasum -a256 confirmation that on-disk artifacts match handoff packet hashes (approval denied)
pytest run output proving the 4 spike tests + ADR test pass GREEN
contents of tests/test_durable_execution_engine_adr.py not read in full (existence + name confirmed via git status)
critical_review.strongest_objection: ADR is marked Status: Accepted while its recommendation is to KEEP hand-rolled, which could read as a non-decision; and artifact sha256 integrity could not be machine-verified because shasum approval was denied.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Discovery of a temporalio import or a live-service dependency in the default path, a workflow-id-on-retry divergence, a reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job signature mismatch making the comparison test vacuous, or a failing pytest run for the spike tests would move this to revise/deny.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: issues_review.

Intent:
Decide via ADR whether Temporal, Restate, or DBOS should replace parts of the durable job stack, with only a disabled report-only Temporal submit spike.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: 4 issues slices cover all PRD promises P1-P4 with no orphans
Every implementation-facing AC maps to a verified source boundary
ISS-2 comparison test exercises the real Layer-0 reservation path (non-vacuous)
Default runtime is unchanged; diff frozen at 14 insertions across config files
No live Temporal SDK is imported anywhere in code

criteria: 4 slices map to PRD P1-P4 with no orphan slices (verified)
Each code-backed AC traces to a real source line (verified)
ISS-2 test calls real State.reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job, non-vacuous (verified)
No state.py / default-runtime change (verified via git status + diff)
evidence: ACCEPT issues_review: 4 slices cover PRD P1-P4 with no orphan slices
ISS-1 ACs source-backed: config.py DurableExecutionCfg defaults (engine=hand_rolled, temporal_spike_enabled=False) + spike.py:78-79 RuntimeError + :81-87 client passthrough + Protocol:18 no-SDK
ISS-2 ACs source-backed and non-vacuous: real State.reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job test:109-130, summary:143-152, default_runtime_changed=False spike.py:130
ISS-3 ACs source-backed: ADR six criteria:28-39, Spike Result:118, Recommendation:198/No Default Runtime Change:185, tests:18-39
ISS-4 is a forward-looking gate/regression validation slice (process ACs), acceptable as validation stage
confidence.rationale: All code-backed ACs verified by direct read of issues.md, config.py diff, spike module, both test files, and ADR section grep; coverage of P1-P4 is complete with non-vacuous Layer-0 test. Held below 0.95 because pytest was not run (test_status unknown) and shasum integrity is self_reported per packet policy, and ISS-4 ACs are forward-looking.

payload.content: issues_review ACCEPT. 4 slices map to PRD P1-P4 with no orphans; every code-backed AC verified at current source (config defaults engine=hand_rolled/temporal_spike_enabled=False, disabled spike raises RuntimeError, client boundary passthrough, real State.reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job comparison non-vacuous, ADR six criteria+spike result+recommendation). Diff frozen config.py +7/config.example.yaml +7, no state.py change.

assumptions_to_verify: Running pytest on the two new test files passes green
The on-disk artifact sha256 values still match the handoff packet
contradictions_checked: Whether any PRD promise is left uncovered by the 4 slices — none; P1-P4 all covered
Whether any slice is orphaned (no PRD promise) — none; all 4 cite PRD promises
Whether 'no live Temporal SDK' AC is contradicted by an import — no temporalio in supervisor/ or tests/
Whether ISS-2 is vacuous/mocked on the Layer-0 side — it uses the real State.reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job
Whether default runtime changed — git status/diff show only config additions, no state.py change
missing_evidence: pytest run output for the 5 tests (test_status unknown)
independent shasum reconfirmation of the 6 planning artifacts (approval not sought; self_reported per policy)
critical_review.strongest_objection: ISS-4's acceptance criteria are process/forward-looking gates (focused tests pass, full suite green, gate accepts through outcome review) that cannot be source-verified at the issues stage, and ISS-1/ISS-2 cite public boundaries (dual_agent_runner, target_config_load) that do not exist as named symbols in the actual tests.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that an AC references a code path that does not exist, that a PRD promise is uncovered, that ISS-2's Layer-0 arm is mocked rather than calling real state, or that the spike/config change actually alters the production submit/default runtime.

Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

dual_agent_runner production submit path remains unmodified (not exercised by spike tests)

critical_review.strongest_objection: Cycle 1 does not exercise the named target_config_load test boundary—only DurableExecutionCfg() direct construction—so YAML load-path regression for durable_execution defaults is unguarded despite working via Field defaults.

assumptions_to_verify: Receipt artifacts are trusted as the current run evidence despite not rerunning tests in this review.
Config.load with durable_execution remains covered indirectly by Config model wiring and existing target config receipt, not by the plan-specific default test.
External Temporal, Restate, and DBOS claims in the ADR are adequate for a report-only decision and will be refreshed before any adoption slice.
contradictions_checked: Checked tests against the TDD plan: all five plan-named tests are present.
Checked Cycle 3 against supervisor/state.py: duplicate idempotency_token returns the existing row with created=False.
Checked production non-test imports: only supervisor/durable_execution_engine_spike.py contains the spike symbols; no temporalio import found outside docs/tests.
Checked ADR against required options, criteria, spike result, replacement/stay boundaries, migration cost, and no-default-change text.
Checked receipts file: it records focused, target-config, full-suite, compileall, and diff-check passes.
missing_evidence: Fresh reviewer-run pytest output was not produced because this review was constrained to read-only inspection.
No live Temporal namespace or SDK compatibility proof exists for USE_EXISTING semantics.
No checksum verification was performed for planning artifacts.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The strongest reason not to advance is that the Temporal side proves only local fake-client argument shape, not live Temporal USE_EXISTING behavior or deployment viability; however the PRD explicitly scopes this slice to a disabled report-only spike and a later live pilot.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A fresh test run failure, a production submit path importing or requiring Temporal, a changed State.reserve duplicate-token behavior that no longer reattaches, or evidence that the ADR recommends default runtime migration would change this to reject.

Cycle 4 ADR test is substring presence only; semantic scoring correctness not exercised
outcome.summary: tdd_review ACCEPT. Independently confirmed all 5 plan-named tests exist verbatim, map to P1-P4/ISS-1-3, exercise real config model, spike module, State.reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job, and ADR file. Focused pytest 5/5 and full suite 734 passed (upgrades Claude test_status unknown). Grill findings 3/3 source-corroborated. Minor boundary-label gaps remain non-blocking.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Decide via ADR whether Temporal, Restate, or DBOS should replace parts of the durable job stack, with only a disabled report-only Temporal submit spike.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: The implementation plan is sound, complete, and fully traceable to current source.
No production submit path (mcp_tools/codex_supervisor_stdio.py) is modified; slice is report-only.
TDD Finding 1 (fake-client could be helper-only) is resolved non-vacuously by the real Layer-0 reservation call.

criteria: File ownership EXACT vs git: verified
Steps map to real symbols: verified
Comparison test hits real Layer-0 boundary (signature match): verified
No temporalio import / MCP submit untouched: verified
pytest executed green: NOT verified (approval denied)
evidence: File ownership is EXACT: 7 declared paths == git changes, no orphans, diff --check clean.
All 5 named traceability tests exist verbatim and map to P1-P4.
Steps 1-4 resolve to real source symbols; Steps 5-6 are process steps.
Comparison test is non-vacuous: calls real State.reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job with a signature matching state.py:829-843 exactly.
confidence.rationale: File ownership, traceability, step-to-symbol mapping, signature compatibility, and ADR string presence are all directly verified against current source; the only unverified item is live pytest execution, which the handoff policy permits as self_reported.

payload.content: Implementation plan accepted: file ownership EXACT (7 declared == git changes, diff --check clean, source diff only config.py+config.example.yaml +14), 5 named traceability tests exist verbatim and map to P1-P4, Steps 1-4 resolve to real symbols (DurableExecutionCfg config.py:149, TemporalSubmitLifecycleSpike spike.py:73, USE_EXISTING:14, ADR 0004 sections), comparison test exercises the real State.reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job (signature matches state.py:829-843 exactly), no temporalio import, MCP submit path untouched.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Decide via ADR whether Temporal, Restate, or DBOS should replace parts of the durable job stack, with only a disabled report-only Temporal submit spike.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: All 6 plan-named traceability tests exist verbatim in the two test files
Comparison test is non-vacuous: invokes real production reservation method with exact signature
Report-only intent enforced: default engine hand_rolled, spike disabled+guarded, default_runtime_changed=False, no temporalio dependency

comparison test calls real reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job with matching sig (verified)
confidence.rationale: File ownership, test existence, symbol mapping, signature match, ADR completeness, and the no-temporalio boundary were all directly verified at current source. Confidence held below 0.95 because pytest and git diff --check were not executed (approval-gated; self_reported evidence grade per handoff policy), so green/RED status is asserted from static inspection rather than a run.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Decide via ADR whether Temporal, Restate, or DBOS should replace parts of the durable job stack, with only a disabled report-only Temporal submit spike.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: The execution gate realizes the report-only ADR intent without changing the production durable-job runtime
The Temporal submit spike is disabled by default and never invoked from any production path
The Layer-0 comparison exercises real reservation code, making the spike comparison non-vacuous

criteria: Default runtime unchanged (config defaults + yaml + default_factory wiring) [met]
Spike disabled-by-default with RuntimeError guard and report_only/default_runtime_changed=False [met]
No temporalio import anywhere in executable code [met]
ADR compares 4 options on 6 criteria with explicit no-default-change recommendation [met]
Comparison test calls real reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job with exact current signature [met]
pytest executed with pass status [not met — un-run, self_reported]
evidence: ACCEPT execution gate: intent realized at current source with no default-runtime change
Default kept hand_rolled: DurableExecutionCfg config.py:149 + config.example.yaml block, wired via default_factory
Spike is disabled-by-default and report-only: RuntimeError at spike.py:78, default_runtime_changed=False
ADR 0004 compares Option A/B/C/D across all 6 criteria and recommends keep hand-rolled with Temporal as only follow-up
Comparison test is non-vacuous: calls real State.reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job, signature-exact state.py:829-843
confidence.rationale: All structural invariants verified directly against current source (config diff, ADR sections, spike guard, no temporalio import, untouched production paths, signature-exact real-boundary call). Confidence capped below 0.95 because pytest was not executed and source shasums were not re-checked, so green is self_reported per packet policy rather than observed.

payload.content: Execution ACCEPT: report-only ADR (Temporal/Restate/DBOS vs keep-hand-rolled) plus disabled-by-default Temporal submit spike, verified at current source. Default runtime unchanged (engine=hand_rolled, spike disabled), no temporalio import in any code, ADR scores 4 options on 6 criteria, comparison test calls the real reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job (signature-exact, non-vacuous), and out-of-scope production paths are untouched.

the sqlite3.Row from the reservation exposes row['recovery_point'] without KeyError
handoff source sha256 values still match on-disk artifacts (mutable_by_worker=false)

missing_evidence: pytest run output with pass/fail per test
re-computed sha256 of the six source planning artifacts matching the handoff packet values
confirmation that sqlite3.Row returned by reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job includes a 'recovery_point' key

payload.content: Independent outcome_review: ADR 0004 satisfies P1–P4 (4 options, 6 criteria, 6 sections, keep-hand-rolled recommendation with Temporal as follow-up only). Spike is disabled-by-default, report-only, no temporalio; production submit unchanged (config-only tracked diff +14; state/stdio clean). Six slice tests and full pytest pass locally; planning artifact shas match outcome-review. Gate should ACCEPT.

assumptions_to_verify: Future slice must wire or explicitly reject durable_execution before any live Temporal pilot
ADR scores remain editorial judgment not mechanically validated beyond string presence
contradictions_checked: Claude test_status unknown vs receipts passed: reran pytest; 6 slice tests + target_config_load green; 735 full suite green
Spike enabled by default? No — TemporalSpikeConfig.enabled=False, temporal_spike_enabled=False, RuntimeError guard
temporalio dependency? No — grep empty in supervisor/ and tests/
Comparison test vacuous? No — calls reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job; duplicate returns existing,False
Production stack modified? No — state/stdio diff empty; only config+yaml tracked (+14)
Planning shas stale? No — recomputed shas match outcome-review.md
missing_evidence: End-to-end test that production submit ignores durable_execution even if engine=temporal_spike (only defaults and isolation grep checked)
critical_review.strongest_objection: durable_execution config and spike module are not referenced by runner or MCP submit code; mis-set YAML would not alter production until a future slice wires the seam.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: pytest failure on slice or full suite; temporalio import; enabled-by-default spike; tracked changes to state.py or codex_supervisor_stdio.py submit path; planning artifact sha mismatch vs handoff/outcome-review

assumptions_to_verify: Tool receipts correspond to this exact worktree snapshot; source receipts and workflow request agree, but I did not execute pytest.
Future slices keep the spike opt-in/report-only until a separate runtime migration is explicitly approved.
External-engine capability summaries in the ADR remain accurate enough for architecture scoring.
contradictions_checked: Default runtime changed? No: DurableExecutionCfg defaults to hand_rolled and temporal_spike_enabled=false, and config.example.yaml matches.
Spike enabled by default or silently active? No: TemporalSpikeConfig.enabled defaults false and start_or_attach raises temporal_spike_disabled.
Hidden Temporal dependency? No: rg for temporalio returned no matches in supervisor/tests/ADR/config.
Production submit path modified? No: git status/diff shows only config tracked changes plus new ADR/spike/tests/artifacts; no state.py or stdio submit-path change.
Comparison test helper-only? No: it calls real State.reserve_dual_agent_workflow_job and matches the current signature.

Receipt contradiction checked: stale transcript fragments with 5 passed are superseded by current source tool-receipts and workflow request showing 6 focused tests, matching the six current test functions.
Planning artifact integrity checked: shasums for PRD/issues/TDD/grill/implementation and grill-findings-tdd match the handoff/manifest values found in source artifacts.
missing_evidence: Raw pytest stdout beyond receipt summaries; I did not rerun pytest in this read-only review.
Fresh external-source citations for Temporal/Restate/DBOS capability claims; this review was codebase-only.
Runtime validation against a real Temporal namespace remains absent, by design for this slice.
A future guard tying engine=temporal_spike to temporal_spike_enabled=true is not present, though no production code consumes it now.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The ADR's replacement-engine scoring depends on high-level product claims and a fake-client Temporal model, not a live Temporal/Restate/DBOS comparison. That would be blocking for a runtime adoption gate, but this slice explicitly keeps production hand-rolled and adds only a disabled report-only spike.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A current failing pytest/compile/diff check, a temporalio import or live Temporal call in the default path, a production submit-path change in state.py or stdio/MCP code, a planning artifact hash mismatch, or external documentation disproving the idempotency/conflict-policy assumptions would move this to revise or deny.

contradictions_checked: Default runtime changed? No: DurableExecutionCfg defaults to hand_rolled and temporal_spike_enabled=false, and config.example.yaml matches.

Production submit path modified? No: git status/diff shows only config tracked changes plus new ADR/spike/tests/artifacts; no state.py or stdio submit-path change.

missing_evidence: Raw pytest stdout beyond receipt summaries; I did not rerun pytest in this read-only review.
Fresh external-source citations for Temporal/Restate/DBOS capability claims; this review was codebase-only.
Runtime validation against a real Temporal namespace remains absent, by design for this slice.

critical_review.strongest_objection: The ADR's replacement-engine scoring depends on high-level product claims and a fake-client Temporal model, not a live Temporal/Restate/DBOS comparison. That would be blocking for a runtime adoption gate, but this slice explicitly keeps production hand-rolled and adds only a disabled report-only spike.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A current failing pytest/compile/diff check, a temporalio import or live Temporal call in the default path, a production submit-path change in state.py or stdio/MCP code, a planning artifact hash mismatch, or external documentation disproving the idempotency/conflict-policy assumptions would move this to revise or deny.
